key_insight: LLM-ACES turns ODE discovery into a closed-loop process where an LLM proposes structured equation priors and active data collection resolves ambiguities in the learned dynamics.
engineering_relevance: The method improves scientific system identification by reducing data needs and recovering more reliable governing equations for control, forecasting, and simulation workflows.
method: LLM suggests operator priors to partition the symbolic search space.
Fit candidate ODEs within each region on observed trajectories.
Use disagreement among candidates to choose the next most informative trajectories for data acquisition.
evidence_signal: Across 122 ODE systems from ODEBench and ODEBase, the method reports the lowest median NMSE, orders-of-magnitude better than baselines, with 46.2% and 52.4% symbolic accuracy and about 10x better data efficiency.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of LLM-generated priors and on acquiring informative new trajectories, so weak proposal guidance or costly experimentation could limit gains in practice.
paper_title: LLM-ACES: Closed-Loop Discovery of Dynamical Systems with LLM-Guided Adaptive Search
